name: Wispr Flow
description: Popular AI dictation app for fast, formatted speak-to-type

developer.comment: San Francisco startup Wispr AI (Wispr), founded 2021 by Tanay Kothari and Sahaj Garg; Flow is its software dictation product.

first-release.comment: Wispr Flow launched Sept 30, 2024 for Mac (Apple Silicon). Windows app followed March 2025, iOS in 2025.

status.comment: Actively developed; expanding platforms and raised $81M to build a 'Voice OS' (checked 2026-06-12).

license.comment: Proprietary closed-source app with a free Basic tier plus paid Pro plan.

primary-type.comment: Pure real-time speak-to-type dictation 'voice keyboard' that types into any app; not a file transcriber or meeting assistant.

real-time-dictation.comment: Core function: press a hotkey, speak, and formatted text appears at the cursor in any app.

file-transcription.comment: No support for importing/uploading audio or video files; reviews recommend other tools (e.g. MacWhisper) for file transcription.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting bot or system/conference audio recording; it is a dictation keyboard, not a meeting recorder.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Batch-style cloud processing: audio is sent on release of the dictation key and text returns in ~1-2s; it does not produce a live running transcript of ongoing audio in the meeting-captions sense.

audio-sources.comment: Microphone input only; no file upload, system-audio, meeting-bot, or URL import.

underlying-engine.value: Cloud pipeline (OpenAI transcription subprocessor + fine-tuned Llama cleanup)
underlying-engine.comment: Vendor does not publish full engine details; reviews describe an OpenAI (Whisper-class) subprocessor for transcription plus a fine-tuned Llama model for filler-word removal, punctuation and formatting.

language-count.comment: Vendor states '100+ languages' with auto-detection; approx (exact count not published).

english-accuracy.comment: Reviews report ~95-97% accuracy on clear English (one review cites ~97.2% WER-equivalent), on par with cloud STT providers, but noticeably degraded by background noise/cross-talk; rated 4.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Automatically detects and switches between multiple languages without manual selection.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Personal custom dictionary plus a snippet library for voice shortcuts; included even on the free tier.

speaker-diarization.comment: Single-speaker dictation tool; no speaker labeling.

timestamps.comment: Outputs inline text into the active app; no word/segment timestamps.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: No meeting summaries or notes; Command Mode does AI text edits/assistant tasks, not transcript summarization.

built-in-editor.comment: No in-app transcript editor; text is dictated directly into whatever app the cursor is in. Command Mode allows voice-driven edits but there is no transcript document to review.

translation.comment: Transcribes in the spoken language; no transcript translation feature documented.

export-formats.comment: No transcript export; dictated text is inserted directly into the target app rather than saved as exportable transcript files.

processing-location.comment: Transcription runs in the cloud (AWS us-east-1) for speed/accuracy; not on-device.

offline-capable.comment: Cloud-only with no offline mode at any tier; internet required for all dictation.

data-used-for-training.comment: Without Privacy Mode, data 'may be used to improve Flow's features and AI models'; enabling Privacy Mode stops model training and retention, so consumer default is opt-out.

data-deletion-control.comment: Privacy Mode (Settings > Data & Privacy) gives zero retention — no audio, transcripts, or edits kept on servers.

platforms.comment: Mac, Windows, iPhone, and Android. No native web or Linux app.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Global hotkey injects text into any focused app (40+ apps cited: Gmail, Slack, VS Code, Notion, ChatGPT, etc.).

integrations.comment: Works inside arbitrary apps via OS-level text injection rather than through named meeting/CRM integrations; no Zoom/Meet/Teams/CRM connectors.

api-automation.comment: No public developer API, CLI, or scripting interface; integration is via OS text injection only.

pricing-model.comment: Free Basic tier plus Flow Pro subscription.

free-tier.comment: Flow Basic is a genuinely usable (not time-limited) free plan.

free-allowance.comment: Includes custom dictionary, 100+ languages, and Privacy Mode. Android temporarily unlimited (limited time). Checked 2026-06-12.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Flow Pro $15/user/month, or $12/month billed annually ($144/yr). Checked 2026-06-12.
